task: Ship v0.2.1 hotfix closing 4 GitHub issues filed against v0.2.0: #5 (daemon path traversal — Unix-side path-traversal portion only; Windows TCP unauthenticated listener deferred to v0.3.0 pending design decision), #11 (vuln SARIF Deserialization findings mislabeled as SqlInjection due to wildcard match arm), #12 (MCP lifecycle handshake fails: server rejects notifications/initialized because id is required and notifications never receive responses), #19 (MCP InitializeResult emits snake_case protocol_version/server_info breaking MCP 2024-11-05 spec-compliant clients incl. Claude Code). All four are confirmed present in HEAD f7cccff (v0.2.0). Sequence: M1 + M2 independent (different files, can parallelize), M3 before M4 (both touch crates/tldr-mcp/src/protocol.rs and server.rs).

release_target.publish: manual (user runs cargo publish; orchestrator outputs commands but does NOT execute)

assertions.evidence: continuum/autonomous/v0.2.1-hotfix/reports/m1-issue-5-daemon-path-traversal.md, continuum/autonomous/v0.2.1-hotfix/validation/m1-daemon-path-traversal.json, commit (see git log: subject 'fix(M1 VAL-001): close #5 — daemon path-traversal validation')
assertions.status_resolution: Reproduction tests `secrets_handler_rejects_absolute_path_outside_project` and `vuln_handler_rejects_absolute_path_outside_project` (file: `crates/tldr-daemon/tests/path_traversal_test.rs`) passed RED on f7cccff with the gate-required symptom — the secrets-test panic message named the leaked-content check (`secrets handler leaked victim file content (root:/canary present in response)`) and dumped the response body showing `"line_content": "password=\"VictimSuperSecretValue_canary_xyz_42\""`, proving the file was read and its contents leaked into the JSON response; the vuln-test panic showed the handler returned `Ok` for an out-of-project absolute path, proving the validation gate was missing. Triage citation `crates/tldr-cli/src/daemon/security.rs:49-56,113-121` was incorrect — the actual file is `crates/tldr-daemon/src/handlers/security.rs`. Fix: wired `tldr_core::validate_file_path(p, Some(&project))` into both unguarded handlers — `secrets` validation at `crates/tldr-daemon/src/handlers/security.rs:53-57` and `vuln` validation at `crates/tldr-daemon/src/handlers/security.rs:120-124` — with `BAD_REQUEST` mapping on validation failure, and dropped the now-unused `PathBuf` import. Both reproduction tests pass GREEN after fix. Full daemon test suite passes (10 lib + 17 integration + 2 path_traversal). Full matrix unchanged at baseline 964/964 (730 exhaustive_matrix + 234 language_command_matrix). Clippy `--workspace --all-features --tests -- -D warnings` clean. 2 source/test files touched (security.rs + new path_traversal_test.rs). Follow-up flagged: audit remaining handlers under `crates/tldr-daemon/src/handlers/{ast,callgraph,flow,quality,search}.rs` for the same unguarded `is_absolute → accept` pattern (recommend one VAL in v0.2.2). Windows TCP unauthenticated listener remains out of scope per contract (deferred to v0.3.0).
assertions.issue: parcadei/tldr-code#5 (path-traversal portion only; Windows TCP auth deferred)
assertions.text: The tldr-daemon IPC handler validates every absolute path argument via tldr_core::validate_file_path before performing any filesystem read, and refuses traversal attempts (e.g. paths containing '..' segments resolving outside the project root, or paths absolute to system locations like /etc/passwd, /Users/<other>/.ssh/, %USERPROFILE%\.ssh on Windows). Triage cited paths at crates/tldr-cli/src/daemon/security.rs:49-56,113-121 — the ACTUAL path is crates/tldr-daemon/src/handlers/security.rs. Worker MUST first verify the file path and locate the unguarded handler entry points (likely the same line region; if line numbers shifted, update them in the worker report). Fix: wire validate_file_path() (exported at tldr_core::validate_file_path per lib.rs:132) into every IPC handler in handlers/security.rs that accepts an absolute path argument. Reproduction test MUST: (1) drive the IPC handler in-process (NOT via shell — keep test deterministic; spawn the daemon in a test thread or invoke the handler function directly with a synthetic JSON request), (2) send a request with path = '/etc/passwd' (or on Windows, the equivalent: 'C:\\Windows\\System32\\drivers\\etc\\hosts'), (3) parse the JSON response, (4) assert the response is an error variant with a recognizable error code/message naming path validation, (5) assert NO field of the response (recursively walk the JSON) contains the string 'root:' or any other line content from the target file. On unfixed HEAD this test MUST fail because the handler returns the file content. RED-REASON GATE: worker MUST paste the literal RED stdout into the milestone report, and that stdout MUST contain either the substring 'root:' (proof the file content actually leaked) OR a panic/assertion failure naming the leaked-content check. If the RED failure is a parse error, harness setup error, or generic 'test failed' without the symptom keyword, the test does NOT exercise the bug — re-roll the test. Hold conditions: if reproduce test passes RED on HEAD before fix → STOP, the path was already validated upstream; if more than 3 distinct handler functions need touching → STOP, broader audit required (defer to v0.3.0). Acceptance: (1) reproduce test passes after fix; (2) cargo test -p tldr-daemon passes; (3) cargo test -p tldr-cli --test exhaustive_matrix --features semantic --release matrix unchanged at baseline (964/964 or whatever current); (4) cargo clippy --workspace --all-features --tests -- -D warnings clean.

assertions.status_resolution: Reproduction tests `vuln_json_labels_deserialization_correctly` and `vuln_sarif_labels_deserialization_correctly` (file: `crates/tldr-cli/tests/vuln_sarif_deserialization_test.rs`) passed RED on f7cccff with the expected symptoms — JSON test failed showing `left: "sql_injection" right: "deserialization"` and SARIF test failed showing `Got rule ids: ["CWE-89"]` (gate-required substrings `sql` and `deserialization` both present). Fix: replaced the wildcard match arm at `crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs:645-651` with an exhaustive `map_core_vuln_type` free function (vuln.rs:705-715) that maps all 6 `tldr_core::security::vuln::VulnType` variants — SqlInjection, Xss, CommandInjection, PathTraversal, Ssrf, Deserialization — with no `_` arm, so future tldr-core variant additions fail to compile until mapped. Pre-fix the wildcard mislabeled BOTH `Ssrf` AND `Deserialization` as SqlInjection. Both reproduction tests pass GREEN after fix. Full matrix unchanged (964/964: 730 exhaustive_matrix + 234 language_command_matrix). Clippy `--workspace --all-features --tests -- -D warnings` clean. 3 source files touched (vuln.rs + new test + new fixture). See `continuum/autonomous/v0.2.1-hotfix/reports/m2-issue-11-vuln-sarif-deserialization.md` for full RED→fix→GREEN narrative.
assertions.text: The `tldr vuln` command, when scanning a source file containing a deserialization sink (Java: ObjectInputStream.readObject() on user-controlled input; Python: pickle.loads on untrusted bytes; Ruby: Marshal.load — worker MUST first verify which language(s) the Deserialization variant fires on by reading crates/tldr-core/src/security/vuln.rs lines 629-712 and choose a fixture in a language that ACTUALLY triggers the rule — Java is suggested but verify), emits SARIF and JSON output in which the finding's vuln_type / SARIF ruleId correctly identifies it as deserialization — NOT as SqlInjection. Root cause confirmed at crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs:645-651: a match arm covers SqlInjection/CommandInjection/Xss/PathTraversal with a wildcard `_ => VulnType::SqlInjection` that silently mislabels any other VulnType variant from tldr_core::security::vuln (verified VulnType::Deserialization exists at vuln.rs:50; VulnType::Ssrf also exists at vuln.rs:60 and would be similarly mislabeled — worker SHOULD audit how many variants are mismapped and either map all of them or explicitly note in the report which remain). Fix: replace the wildcard match with an exhaustive From<tldr_core::security::vuln::VulnType> impl OR an exhaustive match (no wildcard) so the Rust compiler enforces completeness — when tldr_core adds a new VulnType variant, this code fails to compile until it's mapped. Reproduction test MUST: (1) create a fixture file (e.g. crates/tldr-cli/tests/fixtures/deserialize_<lang>/Vuln.<ext>) containing the deserialization sink in whichever language the rule fires on, (2) invoke the vuln command in-process (via the existing test harness pattern) with --format sarif, (3) parse the SARIF JSON, locate runs[0].results[0], (4) assert ruleId corresponds to deserialization (the canonical SARIF ruleId for deserialization in this codebase — worker determines by reading the SARIF emitter and matches its convention), (5) assert ruleId is NOT 'CWE-89' / 'sql-injection' / any sql-injection variant, (6) re-invoke with --format json, parse, assert top-level vuln_type field equals the canonical form for deserialization (e.g. 'deserialization' or 'Deserialization' — match the codebase's convention), and assert it does NOT equal 'sql_injection' / 'sqlinjection' / 'SqlInjection'. On unfixed HEAD this test MUST fail with a wrong ruleId / vuln_type. RED-REASON GATE: worker MUST paste the literal RED stdout into the milestone report, and that stdout MUST contain BOTH (a) the substring 'sql' (case-insensitive — proof the wrong label is being applied) AND (b) the substring matching the deserialization-related expected value (proof the assertion was checking the right thing). If the RED failure is from JSON parse error, fixture not found, vuln command panic on unrelated path, etc., the test is wrong — re-roll. Hold conditions: more than 2 source files touched (vuln.rs + the fixture file is expected; if a new module is needed, flag for review); if the deserialization rule does NOT fire on Java in current code, switch fixture language and document. Acceptance: (1) reproduce test passes after fix; (2) full matrix unchanged; (3) clippy clean; (4) the new exhaustive match would emit a compile-time error if a future tldr_core::security::vuln::VulnType variant is added without a mapping (verify by reading the fix shape — no wildcard arm).

assertions.evidence: continuum/autonomous/v0.2.1-hotfix/reports/m3-issue-12-mcp-lifecycle.md, continuum/autonomous/v0.2.1-hotfix/validation/m3-mcp-lifecycle.json, commit (see git log: subject 'fix(M3 VAL-003): close #12 — MCP lifecycle handshake')
assertions.status_resolution: Three sub-bugs all fixed in one commit on starting HEAD 00ee2dc. (a) `crates/tldr-mcp/src/protocol.rs:31` — `pub id: Value` made `pub id: Option<Value>` with `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` so notification frames (no `id`) deserialize cleanly. (b) `crates/tldr-mcp/src/server.rs::process_request` — refactored signature `String` → `Option<String>`: returns `None` when `request.id.is_none()` (suppressing all response emission for notifications, including the wrong-jsonrpc-version error path and the unknown-method error path); refactored `run()` to skip `writeln!` on `None`. (c) `crates/tldr-mcp/src/server.rs:65` — replaced legacy bare `"initialized"` route with spec-correct `"notifications/initialized"` per MCP 2024-11-05 (the bare form was never spec-correct, NOT kept as alias — would mask client bugs). Files touched: 4 (`crates/tldr-mcp/src/protocol.rs`, `crates/tldr-mcp/src/server.rs`, `crates/tldr-mcp/src/lib.rs` — 1-line scaffold visibility change to expose `process_request` for integration tests, `crates/tldr-mcp/tests/mcp_lifecycle_test.rs` — NEW). Reproduction test (integration) `crates/tldr-mcp/tests/mcp_lifecycle_test.rs` has 4 tests — `notification_frame_emits_no_response`, `lifecycle_handshake_three_frames`, `unknown_notification_method_emits_no_response`, `unknown_request_method_emits_method_not_found`. Plus 1 deserializer-side unit test `protocol::tests::test_parse_notification_has_no_id`. RED on 00ee2dc captured the literal substring `missing field \`id\` at line 1 column 54` (gate (a) ✓) AND a `got 3` frame-count assertion failure (gate (b) ✓ — the unwanted middle frame is the parse-error response to Frame B). GREEN: 4/4 reproduction tests pass; full tldr-mcp suite 49/49 (45 lib + 4 integration); existing `test_parse_valid_request` updated from `request.id == json!(1)` → `request.id == Some(json!(1))` to track the new `Option<Value>` shape (NOT weakened). Matrix unchanged at baseline 964/964 (730 exhaustive_matrix + 234 language_command_matrix). Clippy `--workspace --all-features --tests -- -D warnings` clean. M4 coordination: only `JsonRpcRequest` (M3's struct) touched in `protocol.rs`; `InitializeResult` deliberately left at original snake_case shape — that is M4 VAL-004's territory. The M3 lifecycle test confirms via panic-output dump that the initialize response still contains `protocol_version`/`server_info` snake_case keys, satisfying the M4 pre-condition.
assertions.text: The tldr-mcp server speaks JSON-RPC 2.0 + MCP 2024-11-05 lifecycle correctly: (a) requests with id receive a response, (b) notifications (no id field) MUST NOT receive a response, (c) the canonical client notification 'notifications/initialized' is recognized as a valid (no-op or state-transition) notification rather than rejected as an unknown method or as a malformed request due to missing id. Root cause confirmed at crates/tldr-mcp/src/protocol.rs:31 (`pub id: Value` is non-optional, no #[serde(default)] — so frames without id fail to deserialize), and crates/tldr-mcp/src/server.rs:34-41 (handler always emits a response frame to stdout regardless of incoming id) and :65 (the handler routes the literal method name 'initialized' rather than the spec-correct 'notifications/initialized'). Fix: (a) protocol.rs change `pub id: Value` to `pub id: Option<Value>` with `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`; (b) server.rs route 'notifications/initialized' (in addition to or instead of 'initialized' — worker reads the MCP 2024-11-05 spec to confirm the canonical method name and removes the legacy alias if it was never spec-correct); (c) server.rs: when incoming request id is None, treat as notification — process side effects only, do NOT write any JSON-RPC response frame to stdout. Reproduction test MUST: (1) spawn the MCP server in-process (call into protocol::handle_request or server::dispatch directly with synthetic JSON input — DO NOT spawn the binary via Command::new; cargo test runs debug builds and the test should be portable across Linux/Mac/Windows in case CI eventually picks it up), (2) write three frames to its input in order: Frame A = `{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","id":1,"params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"test","version":"1"}}}`, Frame B = `{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}` (NO id), Frame C = `{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/list","id":2}`; (3) collect ALL emitted output frames (parse each as JSON), (4) assert exactly one frame has `id == 1` (the initialize response), (5) assert NO frame is emitted in response to Frame B — count frames between sending Frame B and sending Frame C, must be zero new frames, (6) assert exactly one frame has `id == 2` (the tools/list response). On unfixed HEAD this test MUST fail at step 5 (or earlier — Frame B might cause a parse error response). RED-REASON GATE: worker MUST paste the literal RED stdout into the milestone report, and that stdout MUST contain at least ONE of: (a) substring 'missing field' / 'missing `id`' (proof step B was rejected as malformed), OR (b) an assertion failure showing more than zero frames emitted between B and C (proof the unwanted response was emitted), OR (c) substring 'unknown method' near 'notifications/initialized' (proof the routing failed). If RED is from compile error, panic in unrelated init, generic 'test failed' without naming the symptom — re-roll the test. Hold conditions: more than 2 files touched (protocol.rs + server.rs); fix accidentally breaks any existing in-tree mcp test (run cargo test -p tldr-mcp to verify); fix introduces a clippy warning. Coordination: M3 must commit BEFORE M4 starts. Both M3 and M4 touch protocol.rs (M3 changes the JsonRpcRequest struct; M4 changes the InitializeResult struct — different structs, but same file). M4 worker must base off M3's commit SHA. Acceptance: (1) reproduce test passes after fix; (2) cargo test -p tldr-mcp passes; (3) full matrix unchanged; (4) clippy clean.

assertions.evidence: continuum/autonomous/v0.2.1-hotfix/reports/m4-issue-19-mcp-camelcase.md, continuum/autonomous/v0.2.1-hotfix/validation/m4-mcp-camelcase.json, commit (see git log: subject 'fix(M4 VAL-004): close #19 — MCP InitializeResult camelCase')
assertions.status_resolution: Reproduction test `initialize_response_uses_camel_case` and `day_one_handshake_responses_have_zero_snake_case_keys` (file: `crates/tldr-mcp/tests/mcp_camelcase_test.rs`) passed RED on starting HEAD `1620b6d` with the gate-required symptoms — both panic messages enumerated `["protocol_version", "server_info"]` as the snake_case keys found in the initialize response, and the full response body dump showed `"protocol_version":"2024-11-05"` and `"server_info":{"name":"tldr-mcp","version":"0.2.0"}` (gate-required substrings `protocol_version` and `server_info` both present). Broader audit finding: `tools_list_response_uses_camel_case` PASSED on the unfixed HEAD — the tools/list response had ZERO snake_case violations after the JSON-Schema-properties exclusion (rationale in report and validation JSON: schema property keys like `exclude_hidden`, `max_results`, etc. are user-defined argument names extracted by tool handlers via `get_optional_*(&args, "<exact_key>")` at `crates/tldr-mcp/src/tools/{ast,callgraph,...}.rs` — they are not MCP-defined response struct fields, and renaming them would silently break every `tools/call` invocation; this is a JSON Schema property declaration, not a wire-format violation). The literal triage line was sufficient. Fix: 2 `#[serde(rename = ...)]` attributes added to `InitializeResult` at `crates/tldr-mcp/src/protocol.rs:158-172` — `#[serde(rename = "protocolVersion")]` on `protocol_version` and `#[serde(rename = "serverInfo")]` on `server_info`; expanded module-level docstring explaining the renames are load-bearing. All 5 reproduction tests pass GREEN after fix (3 sanity + 2 primary). Full tldr-mcp suite passes (45 lib + 5 mcp_camelcase_test + 4 mcp_lifecycle_test = 54 tests). M3's lifecycle test STILL passes — confirms the camelCase rename did not break the lifecycle handshake. Full matrix unchanged at baseline 964/964 (730 exhaustive_matrix + 234 language_command_matrix). Clippy `--workspace --all-features --tests -- -D warnings` clean (two clippy lints fired in the new test code during initial implementation — `clippy::search_is_some` and `clippy::manual_contains` — both refactored to use idiomatic constructs `Iterator::any` then `slice::contains`; no `#[allow]` suppressions used). 2 files touched (`crates/tldr-mcp/src/protocol.rs` + new `crates/tldr-mcp/tests/mcp_camelcase_test.rs`); hard cap of 5 respected. `InitializeParams`/`ClientInfo` (Deserialize-only, incoming params) deliberately left unchanged — out of scope for VAL-004 wire-format-response gate; the silent `.and_then(|p| from_value(p).ok())` swallow at `server.rs:121-124` causes only loss of three eprintln client-info log lines, not handshake failure; flagged for v0.2.2 review.
assertions.issue: parcadei/tldr-code#19 (the only human-filed bug — etal37, filed against v0.1.6 still present in v0.2.0)
assertions.text: The tldr-mcp server's response to ALL standard MCP requests (initialize AND tools/list at minimum — these two are the day-1 handshake exchange that Claude Code performs immediately on connection) emit result-object top-level field names in camelCase per JSON-RPC 2.0 / MCP 2024-11-05 wire spec. Issue #19 was filed by the only human reporter on this run (etal37) and the user-facing failure is 'Claude Code cannot connect to tldr-mcp.' Confirming the literal triage line (renaming protocol_version + server_info on InitializeResult) without verifying the broader response surface risks shipping a half-fix that still leaves Claude Code blocked at the tools/list step. Root cause confirmed at crates/tldr-mcp/src/protocol.rs:151-175: the InitializeResult struct's fields `protocol_version` and `server_info` lack #[serde(rename = ...)] attributes (other peer fields like list_changed, is_error, input_schema were correctly renamed in v0.1.6 work — these two were missed). Fix: (a) add `#[serde(rename = "protocolVersion")]` to the protocol_version field and `#[serde(rename = "serverInfo")]` to the server_info field in InitializeResult; (b) BROADER AUDIT: worker MUST recursively walk the tools/list response (and any other response struct it discovers) and add #[serde(rename = ...)] to EVERY snake_case field — do NOT defer additional fields to a follow-up VAL; if the recursive scan finds snake_case in tools/list result, ToolsListResult, Tool, ServerInfo, ServerCapabilities, or ANY nested struct, fix them in this milestone. The shipping criterion is: ANY response Claude Code could receive during a normal session contains zero snake_case keys at any depth. Reproduction test MUST: (1) spawn the MCP server in-process (same harness pattern as VAL-003), (2) send TWO requests: Frame A = initialize (id=1), Frame B = tools/list (id=2), (3) capture both response frames, (4) parse each as JSON, (5) recursively walk the entire .result object of EACH response, collecting every key at every depth, (6) for both responses: assert NO key matches the regex `^[a-z]+_[a-z]+` (snake_case detector — must not match anywhere in the recursive walk), (7) for the initialize response specifically: assert .result.protocolVersion exists and .result.serverInfo exists. On unfixed HEAD this test MUST fail at step 6 because the initialize response (and likely tools/list response) contain snake_case keys. RED-REASON GATE: worker MUST paste the literal RED stdout into the milestone report, and that stdout MUST contain the substring 'protocol_version' OR 'server_info' OR a snake_case key name from the tools/list audit (proof the recursive walk found a real snake_case key, not a generic test failure). If RED is from JSON parse error, harness panic, or test-not-found, the test does not exercise the bug — re-roll. BROADER-AUDIT GATE: if the tools/list response has zero snake_case keys after fixing only protocol_version+server_info, that's fine and milestone closes. If the tools/list response has snake_case keys, the milestone does NOT close until those are also fixed in this commit (worker may touch additional structs — count those touches against the 5-file cap; if >5 files needed, STOP and escalate). Hold conditions: the new renames break any existing mcp test that asserts on snake_case (if so, that test was wrong — fix it as part of this milestone, do NOT weaken the new test). Coordination: depends on VAL-003 — must rebase on M3's commit. Acceptance: (1) reproduce test passes after fix; (2) the VAL-003 reproduce test still passes (we did not regress the lifecycle handshake); (3) cargo test -p tldr-mcp passes; (4) full matrix unchanged; (5) clippy clean; (6) recursive snake_case walk on initialize+tools/list responses returns zero matches.

assertions.evidence: continuum/autonomous/v0.2.1-hotfix/reports/m5-release-prep.md, continuum/autonomous/v0.2.1-hotfix/validation/m5-release-prep.json, commit a573504 (chore: prep v0.2.1 release), local tag v0.2.1 → a573504
assertions.status_resolution: All worker-scope steps complete: (R1) workspace + 4 crate Cargo.toml versions bumped 0.2.0 → 0.2.1 plus 3 internal path-dep version pins in tldr-cli/Cargo.toml and 2 in tldr-mcp/Cargo.toml + tldr-daemon/Cargo.toml; `cargo check --workspace` cleanly updated Cargo.lock (4 tldr-* entries now show version = "0.2.1"). (R2) CHANGELOG.md updated with ## v0.2.1 — 2026-04-25 section above [0.2.0]; mirrors existing CHANGELOG style (em-dash heading, ### Fixed sub-section, per-issue bullet with commit link, ### Notes); 4 fixes documented (#5 path-traversal Unix-side, #11 vuln SARIF Deserialization+Ssrf, #12 MCP lifecycle 3 sub-bugs, #19 MCP camelCase). (R3) single chore commit a573504 'chore: prep v0.2.1 release' — staged explicitly (Cargo.toml × 5, Cargo.lock, CHANGELOG.md only — 7 files, 30 insertions, 14 deletions); the prior-orchestrator artifacts in continuum/autonomous/issue-1-bug-fixes/* + various untracked dirs deliberately left unstaged per scope constraint. (R5-prep) annotated tag v0.2.1 created locally pointing to commit a573504 with message 'v0.2.1 — hotfix release closing #5, #11, #12, #19'. (R6) 4 cargo publish commands output as plain text in the kraken final report and in the handoff (DEPENDENCY ORDER: tldr-core first; tldr-mcp + tldr-daemon next, parallelizable; tldr-cli last — verified by reading each crate's Cargo.toml internal path deps). (R7) 4 issue-closing comment drafts output as plain text in the kraken final report and the handoff (#5, #11, #12, #19); each comment links the milestone fix commit SHA, the milestone report path, the test path, and the v0.2.1 release URL. Handoff yaml written at thoughts/shared/handoffs/v0.2.1-shipped/2026-04-25_<HHMM>_v0.2.1-released.yaml mirroring v0.2.0 handoff structure, with next_session_prompt referencing Run 2 (v0.2.2 quality release covering issues #6, #8, #9, #10, #13, #14, #15, #16, #18). DEFERRED to user per orchestrator override (consistent with v0.2.0 manual cargo publish pattern): (R4) `git push origin main` (one new commit a573504 since origin/main; total 5 commits ahead of origin), (R5-push) `git push origin v0.2.1` to fire cargo-dist GitHub Release build, and (R6-execute) `cargo publish -p <crate>` × 4. The kraken report explicitly labels these as user-side actions and provides the verbatim command sequence. No `git push`, no `git push --tags`, no `cargo publish`, no `gh issue comment` was executed by the agent.
assertions.text: v0.2.1 release shipped: (R1) workspace + 4 crate Cargo.toml versions bumped 0.2.0 → 0.2.1, (R2) CHANGELOG.md updated with ## [0.2.1] section dated today (or next day if work spans midnight) listing the 4 fixes by issue number with one-line descriptions, (R3) single chore commit `chore: prep v0.2.1 release`, (R4) push to main, (R5) tag v0.2.1, push tag — cargo-dist via .github/workflows/release.yml builds 4 platform binaries automatically. (R6) STOP — DO NOT cargo publish. Output 4 `cargo publish -p <crate>` commands as plain text for the user to run manually (matches v0.2.0 process per handoff). (R7) Draft 4 issue-closing comments (one per #5 #11 #12 #19) — each links the milestone-fix commit SHA, the milestone report, and the v0.2.1 release URL — output as plain text for user to paste, do NOT auto-post. Acceptance: (1) git tag v0.2.1 exists and points to the chore commit; (2) git push --tags succeeded; (3) the 4 publish commands output to user; (4) the 4 issue comment drafts output to user; (5) handoff written at thoughts/shared/handoffs/v0.2.1-shipped/{timestamp}_v0.2.1-released.yaml mirroring the v0.2.0 handoff structure with a next_session_prompt referencing Run 2 (v0.2.2 quality release covering issues #6, #8, #9, #10, #13, #14, #15, #16, #18).

Every IPC handler in crates/tldr-daemon/src/handlers/ that accepts an absolute path argument from the caller validates that path via tldr_core::validate_file_path before performing any filesystem read. M1 fixed this for the two handlers in security.rs (secrets, vuln). M6 audits the remaining handler files: ast.rs, callgraph.rs, flow.rs, quality.rs, search.rs (per the directory structure surfaced earlier). For each: identify every handler function that takes a path argument as input; check whether that path is currently routed through validate_file_path before any std::fs::read / File::open / similar; if not, fix in this milestone using the same pattern M1 established (call validate_file_path with project root context, return BAD_REQUEST on validation failure). Worker MUST first enumerate the audit set: list every handler function across the 5 files that takes a path argument. Worker then writes ONE reproduction test PER unguarded handler discovered (mirror the M1 pattern from path_traversal_test.rs — synthetic out-of-project absolute path, assert error response + no leaked content). RED-REASON GATE: each test's RED stdout MUST contain the leaked content keyword (the canary string from the victim file) OR a panic naming the leaked-content check. If audit finds ZERO unguarded handlers across the 5 files: that's a valid outcome — milestone closes with status_resolution noting 'audit complete, no additional handlers required fixing; M1's fix was complete for the discoverable surface'. Acceptance: (1) every handler in handlers/{ast,callgraph,flow,quality,search}.rs that takes a path arg either (a) was found to already validate, or (b) has a new validation call + a passing reproduction test. (2) cargo test -p tldr-daemon passes. (3) full matrix unchanged. (4) clippy clean. STOP conditions: more than 6 unguarded handlers found across the 5 files (suggests a systemic gap requiring design discussion — escalate); fix requires touching tldr-core (escalate, may interact with other v0.2.2 work); >5 source files touched (excluding tests, fixtures, contract, reports).

assertions.evidence: continuum/autonomous/v0.2.1-hotfix/reports/m7-mcp-initialize-params-audit.md, continuum/autonomous/v0.2.1-hotfix/validation/m7-mcp-initialize-params-audit.json, commit (see git log: subject 'fix(M7 VAL-007): close #19 request-side audit — accept camelCase params')
assertions.status_resolution: Audit of `crates/tldr-mcp/src/protocol.rs` request-side structs (every Deserialize-deriving struct used as a `params` type in the server dispatch). 5 structs examined: `JsonRpcRequest` (M3 territory, no snake_case fields — OK), `InitializeParams` (params for `initialize` — TWO snake_case fields `protocol_version` and `client_info` without rename; FIXED), `ClientCapabilities` (single-word `experimental` field — OK), `ClientInfo` (single-word `name`/`version` fields — OK), `ToolsCallParams` (params for `tools/call` — single-word `name`/`arguments` fields — OK). 10 Serialize-only response structs (`JsonRpcResponse`, `JsonRpcError`, `InitializeResult`, `ServerCapabilities`, `ToolsCapability`, `ServerInfo`, `ToolsListResult`, `ToolDefinition`, `ToolsCallResult`, `ContentItem`) excluded as M4 territory. Total: 1 struct needed fixing, 2 fields affected. Fix: added `#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]` to `InitializeParams` at `crates/tldr-mcp/src/protocol.rs:142` (struct-level form chosen over per-field rename to cover both fields in one attribute and to be future-proof against new fields silently regressing) plus expanded module docstring documenting the load-bearing nature of the rename. Closes the deferral note recorded in M4's status_resolution. Reproduction test `crates/tldr-mcp/tests/mcp_request_params_test.rs` has 3 tests: PRIMARY (`initialize_params_accepts_camelcase_keys`) parses the canonical camelCase payload directly and asserts every field is populated; SUPPLEMENTARY (`initialize_request_via_process_request_accepts_camelcase_params`) drives the same payload through `server::process_request` end-to-end; NEGATIVE-CONTROL (`initialize_params_snake_case_keys_no_longer_bind_post_fix`) confirms the rename is exclusive (snake_case keys no longer bind post-fix) — documenting the deliberate breaking change for any non-conforming legacy client per contract: 'the spec is the contract.' RED on starting HEAD `a573504` captured the literal substring `parsed.protocol_version was None despite protocolVersion="2024-11-05" being sent in JSON` (RED-REASON gate satisfied: panic names a specific dropped field `protocol_version`, the camelCase key sent `protocolVersion`, AND the full payload reproducing the second dropped key `clientInfo`). The supplementary test passes RED — that is the silent-loss bug surface in action: `process_request` returns a successful initialize response while internally never seeing the announced protocol version or client info. GREEN post-fix: 3/3 reproduction tests pass. Full tldr-mcp suite passes (45 lib + 5 mcp_camelcase_test + 4 mcp_lifecycle_test + 3 mcp_request_params_test = 57/57); M3 lifecycle test STILL passes; M4 camelcase test STILL passes (no regression in either direction). Full matrix unchanged at baseline 964/964 (730 exhaustive_matrix + 234 language_command_matrix). Clippy `--workspace --all-features --tests -- -D warnings` clean. 1 source file touched (`crates/tldr-mcp/src/protocol.rs`) + 1 new test file (`crates/tldr-mcp/tests/mcp_request_params_test.rs`); both well within the implicit hard cap. M6 disjoint: M6 touches `crates/tldr-daemon/src/handlers/*` only; M7 touches `crates/tldr-mcp/src/protocol.rs` only — zero file overlap. M8 not preempted: no Cargo.toml or CHANGELOG.md changes (those are M8's job).
assertions.issue: parcadei/tldr-code#19 (request-side audit — extends M4's response-side fix)
assertions.text: The tldr-mcp server accepts inbound MCP requests whose params use camelCase per MCP 2024-11-05 wire spec, AND the request structs in crates/tldr-mcp/src/protocol.rs that are deserialized from client input use camelCase #[serde(rename = ...)] attributes (or natively-camelCase field names) to match the spec — so the server doesn't silently drop fields a spec-compliant client (e.g. Claude Code) sends. M4 fixed the response-side (InitializeResult). M7 audits the request-side: every struct in protocol.rs that is deserialized from incoming client params (InitializeParams, possibly CallToolParams, etc.) must accept camelCase keys. Step 0: read protocol.rs end-to-end. Identify every struct deserialized from client request params (hint: structs used as `params` types in JsonRpcRequest dispatch, structs with `Deserialize` derive that are NOT `Serialize`-only-response-shapes). For each, list the fields that use snake_case naming and check whether they have #[serde(rename = ...)] or #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] applied. Worker writes a reproduction test that sends an `initialize` request with all params in camelCase (`{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"test","version":"1"}}`), drives it through the server's deserialize step, and asserts the parsed InitializeParams has all expected fields populated (NOT defaulted to None/empty due to a missing rename). RED-REASON GATE: the RED stdout MUST contain a specific field name that came back as None/default when the corresponding camelCase key was sent (e.g. `parsed.client_info was None despite clientInfo being sent`). If RED is from compile error / harness panic / generic test fail without naming a missing field — re-roll. Fix: add #[serde(rename = ...)] OR #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] to every audit-discovered struct that needs it. Acceptance: (1) audit complete: every params-side struct in protocol.rs is documented as either correctly-camelCase or fixed in this milestone; (2) reproduction test passes after fix; (3) M3's mcp_lifecycle_test still passes; (4) M4's mcp_camelcase_test still passes; (5) cargo test -p tldr-mcp passes; (6) full matrix unchanged; (7) clippy clean. STOP: more than 4 structs need fixing (suggests a wider redesign — escalate); fix breaks existing client compatibility for any non-spec-conforming legacy clients (document but proceed — the spec is the contract).

v0.2.1 release re-prepared with expanded scope (original 4 fixes + 2 audit-driven fixes from M6 + M7). Local v0.2.1 tag has been deleted by orchestrator before this milestone runs. Worker: (R1) verify all 4 crate versions are still 0.2.1 (no second bump needed — the M5 chore commit a573504 already did this; no additional version change). (R2) UPDATE CHANGELOG.md `## v0.2.1 — 2026-04-25` section to add the M6 + M7 fixes (or whatever subset of M6/M7 actually produced commits — if M6 audit found zero unguarded handlers and made no commit, do NOT add a CHANGELOG bullet for it; if M7 audit found zero issues, same). The CHANGELOG section must reflect what actually shipped, not what was audited. (R3) ONE new chore commit `chore: v0.2.1 release prep (expanded scope)`. (R5-prep) annotated tag v0.2.1 created locally pointing to this new chore. (R6) regenerate the 4 cargo publish commands (same as M5 — dependency order). (R7) regenerate / extend the 4 issue-closing comment drafts; if M6 produced additional fix evidence relevant to #5, the #5 comment notes it; if M7 produced additional fix evidence relevant to #19, the #19 comment notes it. Write final handoff at thoughts/shared/handoffs/v0.2.1-shipped/{timestamp}_v0.2.1-released-expanded.yaml superseding the M5 handoff (move or delete the M5 handoff yaml — the expanded one is now the canonical record). Acceptance: (1) git tag v0.2.1 (recreated) points to the new chore commit; (2) CHANGELOG accurately lists every commit that ships under v0.2.1 (4 originals + however-many M6/M7 produced); (3) the 4 publish commands + 4 (or amended) issue comment drafts output to user; (4) handoff yaml written. DO NOT push, DO NOT cargo publish, DO NOT post issue comments — same orchestrator override as M5.
